=Template syntax=

Translations are defined in a TSV file. The first column is the key
(used as the OCaml function name). The following columns are the
translations for each language, in the order specified by the
{{{--languages}}} option.

==Simple strings

{{{
foo	This is a simple key.	Ceci est une clé toute simple.
}}}

==Variables

{{{{{x}}}}} defines a labelled argument {{{~x}}}:

{{{
bar	I am {{x}}.	Je suis {{x}}.
}}}

==Typed variables

{{{{{x %d}}}}} defines a labelled argument {{{~x}}} with a format specifier:

{{{
bu	I am {{x %s}} ({{n %d}}).	Je suis {{x %s}} ({{n %d}}).
}}}

==Conditional values

{{{{{{{c?if_true||if_false}}}}}} defines an optional boolean argument
{{{?(c=false)}}}.

{{{
baz	There {{{c?are||is}}} apple{{{c?s||}}} here!	Il y a {{{c?des||une}}} pomme{{{c?s||}}} ici !
}}}

==Notes

* Languages do not need to use the same variables. The compiler
  generates a function taking all parameters it detects across all
  languages.
* A variable name used twice refers to the same argument.
* The TSV file must end with a newline.
